what_it_does: Acceptance test for the C2 teach-to-request-change bootstrap. It exercises the guide organ over a registry-true UI element flow (point, ask, surface, approve), checks model-free teach narration and an indicate hint on the point step, verifies end-to-end next() progression, enforces fail-loud behavior for empty topics and side-channel length mismatches, and includes regression checks that the default C1 tour remains unchanged when teach/indicate are absent.
observations: The module docstring frames C2 as a named guided sequence reusing the C1 organ with parallel GUIDE_TEACH and GUIDE_INDICATE side-channels.
It defines POINT, ASK, and INBOX as ui:// address constants and asserts the request-a-change sequence matches them exactly.
It calls su.start_guide('request-a-change') bare and checks organ_started, session id, cursor, done, item, mode, and steps.
It loads the session from the store and asserts items are address strings while teach and indicate are aligned lists of length 4.
present_current is used to inspect step 0 framing, raw.ui_target, raw.guide_address, raw.teach, and raw.indicate.
It advances through the sequence with next(), checking each step's item, ui_target, framing content, and absence of indicate after step 0.
It asserts the approve step framing is fidelity-true to a safe-by-default/plan-mode model.
It tests fail-loud behavior by injecting an empty guide sequence and by passing mismatched teach/indicate lengths to start_session.
Regression checks confirm the default tour session has no teach/indicate keys and its framing equals the corpus how-to.
It sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1 for inbox-hygiene parity with sibling suites.
standouts: The test hardcodes the expected UI address flow and treats it as the source of truth for the bootstrap.
It asserts specific natural-language phrases in framing strings, including safe-by-default wording on the approve step.
It validates side-channel alignment both by persisted session shape and by explicit ValueError checks.
conventions: Follows the showme acceptance-test pattern with a global PASS counter and a check() helper that exits on failure.
Uses a fresh_suite() factory to isolate each section with a temporary FsStore and freshly discovered NodeRegistry.
Directly invokes internal/private Suite methods such as _guide_sequence, _registry_howt_for, and start_session.
Sets an environment flag to coordinate test behavior with sibling suites.
concerns: The test is tightly coupled to internal implementation details (raw field names, session shape, private methods).
It hardcodes UI addresses and expected copy/phrasing, making it brittle to registry or wording changes.
Asserting exact natural-language output risks frequent breakage when corpus text evolves.
notes: This is the acceptance harness that proves the C2 bootstrap by walking the real request-a-change guided sequence end-to-end.
questions: Where are GUIDE_SEQUENCES, GUIDE_TEACH, and GUIDE_INDICATE defined and how is the 'self-modify' alias configured?
What is the exact FE contract for raw.teach and raw.indicate, and how does indicate differ from guide_address?
How does _registry_howto_for relate to address_help for the default tour corpus check?
Are teach and indicate persisted in the session record for all guided sessions or only when explicitly supplied?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it proves the C2 teach-to-request-change bootstrap by use and guards C1 behavior.
intention_signals: It aims to show that the existing C1 guided-sequence organ can be reused to teach operators how to request changes, layering teach and indicate side-channels without changing the underlying session shape.
novelty: Treats a tutorial as a first-class, system-startable guided sequence over live UI element addresses, using side-channel narration and gating hints to mount transient UI affordances.
contribution: A concrete acceptance contract for the C2 bootstrap that preserves the C1 organ shape while adding teach/indicate side-channels, and documents expected UI addresses, narration content, and failure modes.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for the C2 teach-to-request-change guided sequence that verifies registry-true UI step binding, model-free teach narration, indicate hints, end-to-end next() progression, fail-loud errors, and C1 regression behavior.
intention_for_embedding: To prove by use that the C1 guide organ can bootstrap operators through the request-a-change flow without invoking a model and without breaking the existing default tour.

symbols.does: Test helper that increments PASS on a true condition or exits the process on failure.
symbols.description: A simple assertion helper used throughout the acceptance test; it prints PASS/FAIL per label and raises SystemExit(1) immediately on failure, making the test script fail fast.

symbols.does: Constructs a fresh Suite with a temporary FsStore and a NodeRegistry loaded from the nodes directory.
symbols.description: Factory used before each test section to create an isolated Suite instance, ensuring clean sessions and registry state for the C2 acceptance checks.
